title: Special Mortgage Programs
metaTitle: Special Mortgage Programs Canada — Family Gift, Co-Signor, Niche
metaDescription: Specialty mortgage programs for unique situations: family-gifted down payments, co-signor and guarantor structures, lender-specific niche products in BC.
intro: Some files don't fit a category — they fit a specific lender's specific program. These are the niche solutions that experienced brokers know exist but most websites don't list.

body.heading: Family gift programs
paragraphs: Most A-lenders accept gifted down payments from immediate family with a signed gift letter and verification of source. A handful go further with structured gift programs that allow the gifter to remain on title or as a guarantor without the standard liability.

body.heading: Co-signor and guarantor programs
paragraphs: Co-signor: full liability, on title and on mortgage. Guarantor: secondary liability, often not on title. A few lenders offer guarantor-only programs for parents helping children — limiting the parent's liability while still strengthening the application.

body.heading: Lender-specific niches
paragraphs: Examples: programs for active military and RCMP members; aboriginal home-ownership programs (RBC, BMO, several credit unions); low-deposit programs for specific corporate employer relationships; NHA Multi-Family programs with preferential terms for affordable rental developments. The list is longer than this page can hold — if you have an unusual situation, ask. There's often a program.

whoItsFor: Have a unique situation that doesn't fit standard categories
Have access to family financial help and want it structured efficiently
Are part of a profession or community with specific lender programs

faqs.question: How do I find out which programs apply to me?
faqs.answer: A 20-minute call. Tell me your situation in plain English; I'll match it to the lenders whose programs fit.
